story.iWant: to edit the details of an existing lesson
story.soThat: I can correct errors or update content

task.title: Create LessonEdit container structure
task.subtask: 1.1 Create src/containers/Main/LessonEdit/ directory
1.2 Create connect.ts with form setup, pre-populated values, and mutation logic
1.3 Create constants.ts - reuse validation schema from LessonCreate
1.4 Create types.ts with LessonEditProps interface (including lesson data)
1.5 Create index.tsx with modal dialog and pre-populated form UI

task.title: Implement form pre-population logic
task.subtask: 2.1 Accept lesson prop with current lesson data
2.2 Set form defaultValues from lesson data
2.3 Map lesson fields to form schema (handle field name differences if any)
2.4 Initialize tone dropdown with current lesson tone value

task.title: Implement update mutation in connect.ts
task.subtask: 3.1 Implement $api.useMutation('put', '/api/courses/{slug}/lessons/{id}')
3.2 Handle onSuccess: invalidate course query, show toast, close modal
3.3 Handle onError: show error toast with message
3.4 Implement dirty state check - skip API call if no changes (AC #5)
3.5 Compare current values vs default values to detect changes

task.title: Implement validation and error handling
task.subtask: 4.1 Reuse lessonSchema from LessonCreate/constants.ts
4.2 Configure zodResolver with same validation rules
4.3 Focus first invalid field on validation failure
4.4 Display inline errors using FormMessage component

task.title: Implement unsaved changes confirmation
task.subtask: 5.1 Track dirty state with formState.isDirty
5.2 Intercept Cancel button click - show confirmation if dirty
5.3 Intercept Escape key - show confirmation if dirty
5.4 Intercept outside click - show confirmation if dirty
5.5 Reuse AlertDialog pattern from LessonCreate

task.title: Integrate with CourseDetail container
task.subtask: 6.1 Add edit modal state and selected lesson to CourseDetail/connect.ts
6.2 Pass onEdit handler to LessonList component
6.3 Pass selected lesson data to LessonEdit modal
6.4 Wire up LessonEdit modal open/close handlers

task.title: Testing and validation
task.subtask: 7.1 Test modal opens with pre-populated data (all fields)
7.2 Test form submission with valid changed data
7.3 Test form submission with no changes (verify no API call)
7.4 Test form submission with invalid data (verify inline errors)
7.5 Test unsaved changes confirmation (Cancel, Escape, outside click)
7.6 Verify list updates after successful edit
7.7 Run bun typecheck and bun lint:fix

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given I see the lesson list, When I click the edit icon (✏️) on a lesson row, Then a modal opens with the form pre-populated with current data.

Given the edit modal is open, Then all fields are editable: Title (current value shown), Duration in minutes (current value shown), Core Practice (current value shown), Key Point (current value shown), Tone (current value shown in dropdown).
Given I modify fields and click "Save Changes", Then: The changes are saved to the backend, A success toast shows: "Lesson updated", The list updates with new data, The modal closes.
Given I submit with invalid data, Then: Inline validation errors appear below each invalid field, The form does not submit, Focus moves to the first invalid field.
Given I haven't made changes and click "Save", Then the modal closes without API call.
Given I have unsaved changes and click "Cancel" or press Escape, Then: A confirmation dialog appears: "Discard changes?", If confirmed modal closes without saving, If cancelled modal remains open.

doc.title: Senda CMS Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Containers have connect.ts for ALL logic (API calls, forms, effects). Components are pure presentational. Separation: index.tsx (UI component) + connect.ts (data logic, forms, mutations) + types.ts (local types) + constants.ts (Zod schemas).

doc.title: Senda CMS Architecture Document
doc.section: Form Management: React Hook Form + Zod
doc.snippet: Use React Hook Form with zodResolver for validation. Zod schemas defined in constants.ts. Form components use FormField, FormItem, FormLabel, FormControl, FormMessage from shadcn/ui.

doc.title: Senda CMS Architecture Document
doc.snippet: All API interactions use auto-generated hooks from openapi-react-query. Use $api.useMutation('put', '/api/courses/{slug}/lessons/{id}') for update operations. Invalidate queries on success.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Medium modal: 600px max-width for most forms. Dismiss with click outside (with unsaved changes warning), Escape key, or close button. Focus trapped within modal. Return focus to trigger on close.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Validation onBlur for most fields. Error display with red border + message below. Required fields marked with asterisk. Help text connected via aria-describedby.

doc.title: Epic 3: Lesson Management
doc.snippet: Modal-based editing with pre-populated form. Reuse LessonForm component from Story 3.2. Use PATCH mutation with optimistic update for instant feedback.

file.reason: REUSE these validation schema and constants for LessonEdit - exact same validation rules apply
REFERENCE pattern for form setup with zodResolver, mutation handling with toast, and discard confirmation dialog flow
REFERENCE pattern for Dialog modal structure, Form fields layout, AlertDialog for discard confirmation
Already has onEdit callback prop wired up - edit button calls onEdit(lesson) when clicked
Already accepts onEditLesson prop and passes it to LessonListItem as onEdit
MODIFY to add edit modal state (isLessonEditOpen, selectedLesson) and handlers (handleEditLesson, handleCloseLessonEdit)
MODIFY to pass onEditLesson to LessonList and render LessonEdit modal with selected lesson
Use Lesson type for pre-populating form, UpdateLessonData for mutation body

node.usage: Form state management with useForm hook

constraints.constraint: Container Pattern: All form logic, mutations, and state in connect.ts. index.tsx is purely presentational.
OpenAPI-First: Use $api.useMutation('put', '/api/courses/{slug}/lessons/{id}') - never manual fetch calls.
Types from models.ts: Import Lesson from @/types/models, not directly from api.d.ts.
Reuse lessonSchema from LessonCreate/constants.ts - do NOT duplicate validation logic.
Run bun typecheck before committing - TypeScript strict mode with noUncheckedIndexedAccess enabled.
Modal traps focus when open, Escape closes (with confirmation if dirty), submit button disabled during pending state.
Skip API call if form not dirty (AC #5) - optimize by checking formState.isDirty before mutation.
Unsaved changes confirmation uses AlertDialog component for consistent UX with LessonCreate.

interface.name: Update Lesson API
LessonEditProps
Query Invalidation Keys

tests.standards: Per project standards, testing includes: TypeScript validation (bun typecheck), ESLint (bun lint:fix), and manual verification of acceptance criteria. No automated unit test framework currently configured.
tests.locations: Manual testing in development environment. Run bun dev, navigate to course detail page, test edit flow.

ideas.idea: Verify edit button click opens modal with all fields pre-populated with lesson data.
Verify all form fields are editable and show current values (title, duration, corePractice, keyPoint, tone dropdown).
Verify submitting changed data: API call made, success toast appears, list refreshes with new data, modal closes.
Verify validation: clear title field, submit, see inline error, focus on title input.
Verify no-change submission: open modal, click Save without changes, modal closes without API call (check Network tab).
Verify discard confirmation: change title, click Cancel, see "Discard changes?" dialog, click Discard, modal closes.
Verify discard confirmation cancel: change title, click Cancel, see dialog, click Keep Editing, modal stays open with changes.
Verify Escape key triggers discard confirmation when form is dirty.
Verify keyboard navigation: Tab through form fields, Enter submits form.
Run bun typecheck - should pass with no errors.
Run bun lint:fix - should pass with no errors.
